title: Achasma pavieanum
taxonomy: Achasma pavieanum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It is native to Madagascar and belongs to the genus Achasma. This genus is part of the subfamily Rauvolfioideae. The classification of Achasma pavieanum reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Apocynaceae family. Further research and updates may be needed to confirm the classification of this species.

intro: Achasma pavieanum is a plant species in the Apocynaceae family, commonly known as the "Dragon's Blood" plant due to its reddish sap. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it beneficial for wound healing, pain relief, and protection against infections.
